findings.evidence: mapPoints_apply (line 64) and pointsFunctor_map_apply (line 138) share the identical RHS `toConv (φ.hom.comp f.ofConv)`; the others are `mapPoints_one/_mul/_inv` composed with the line-130 simp bridge.
findings.fix: Keep one path: retain `pointsFunctor_map` plus the `mapPoints_*` lemmas and drop the four `pointsFunctor_map_{apply,one,mul,inv}` restatements. Keep `pointsFunctor_map_apply_apply` (143) — it is the only genuinely new pointwise form; consider adding the analogous `mapPoints` pointwise lemma instead if `mapPoints` is meant to be primary.
findings.issue: Duplicated morphism API. `@[simp] pointsFunctor_map` (line 130) rewrites `pointsFunctor.map φ` to `mapPoints φ`, so `pointsFunctor_map_apply` (136), `pointsFunctor_map_one` (151), `pointsFunctor_map_mul` (156), and `pointsFunctor_map_inv` (163) are just the `mapPoints_apply/_one/_mul/_inv` lemmas (62/83/88/93) re-expressed and add no new content.

findings.evidence: Lines 129-132 and 135-139 both rewrite occurrences of `(pointsFunctor (H := H)).map φ`.
findings.fix: Mark only one as `@[simp]` (recommend `pointsFunctor_map` as the bridge, leaving `pointsFunctor_map_apply` un-annotated or removed per the finding above).
findings.issue: Non-confluent simp normal form: both `pointsFunctor_map` (@[simp], line 130 → `mapPoints φ`) and `pointsFunctor_map_apply` (@[simp], line 135 → `toConv (...)`) target `pointsFunctor.map φ`, giving two competing normal forms for the same term.

summary: The group and functor characteristic API is complete, annotated, and keeps bodies hidden, but the morphism API is duplicated: with the @[simp] bridge pointsFunctor_map, the pointsFunctor_map_{apply,one,mul,inv} lemmas are simp-provable restatements of the mapPoints_* lemmas, and two @[simp] lemmas compete for the same LHS.
